title: Add steps to sign included qemu and notarize the built pkg
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

- This adds the missing signing of the qemu binary included in the pkg
- adds makefile target `notarize` to perform notarization of the built pkg installer

#### To test generation of signed and notarized pkg artifact run

```
$ make ARCH=<amd64 | aarch64> CODESIGN_IDENTITY=<ID> PRODUCTSIGN_IDENTITY=<ID> NOTARIZE_USERNAME=<appleID> NOTARIZE_PASSWORD=<appleID-password> notarize
```

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None

```

comments.body: @benoitf @gbraad This handles the signing for the `qemu` binary and the needed notarization, however i am not able to test it for lack of a key
LGTM, but way out of my wheelhouse.  I'll lean on @ashley-cui for this one.

Updated the PR as suggested, fixed the pkg name issue, it should've been `PGK_NAME` instead of `PKG_ARCHIVE_NAME` which i forgot to update earlier, `PKG_ARCHIVE_NAME` was not defined in the Makefile, should be good now.

right, i suspected it'd need everything to be signed but wanted to be a bit conservative before taking that step

so now the script is updated to sign almost everything (anything that has executable permissions, named as '*.dylib' and not a symlink) inside `Contents/Payload/opt/podman/qemu/{bin,lib}`

please give it another try

Signed and notarize worked for me! Stapled and validated too. Thanks @anjannath! 
Re-started a flake, then we can merge :)

/lgtm
/hold
are you able to publish the signed pkg somewhere so people can experiment it as well ?

Oops. One more review, since I forgot to test with amd64. 

@benoitf, github won't allow me to attach .pkg files to PR's, I'll put them in the slack channel, once the amd64 version is fixed :)
@ashley-cui slack works for me (on github you can also zip/tar the file so it can be attached but it needs to be < 25MB)

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *anjannath*, *ashley-cui* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [ashley-cui] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
